articles.title: Thane Water Crisis: Dam Levels Drop To Around 28% Amid Delayed Monsoon - lokmattimes.com
articles.full_text:
Thane district is facing a severe water crisis as delayed monsoon rains and an intense heatwave linked to the El Nino effect have reduced water levels in major reservoirs to around 28% capacity. Temperatures touching 42°C have increased evaporation, leaving authorities with only 28 to 50 days of available water reserves.
Major Dams At Low Storage Levels
According to the latest figures, key reservoirs supplying water to the Mumbai Metropolitan Region are operating at low storage levels.
Current Water Stock
Bhatsa: 264.70 MCM (28.10% capacity)Modaksagar: 23.82 MCM (18.84% capacity)Middle Vaitarna: 22.31 MCM (11.53% capacity)How Thane Gets Its Water
Thane city's daily water supply depends on multiple agencies.
The city's daily supply includes:
Thane Municipal Corporation (TMC): 250 MLDMaharashtra Industrial Development Corporation (MIDC): 135 MLDSTEM Authority: 115 MLDBrihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC): 90 MLDAuthorities Restructure Water Supply
To manage the shortage, the STEM Authority and MIDC have started staggered temporary shutdowns to reduce pressure on the water distribution system.
The move is aimed at managing the available water more efficiently until reservoir levels improve.

Residents Feel The Impact
The shortage has already affected several parts of Thane, especially residential areas along Ghodbunder Road, where many housing societies are increasingly relying on private water tankers.
Authorities have appealed to citizens to use water carefully and conserve available supplies, warning that the situation could worsen if adequate rainfall is not received.
